city_report.transport: I tram sono il mezzo più rapido per spostarsi da un punto all'altro della città. È possibile utilizzare autobus e tram con lo stesso biglietto entro un'ora.

attraction.name: Grand Place
attraction.description: Central square known for its ornate guildhouses.

attraction.name: Atomium
attraction.description: Futuristic structure representing an iron crystal.

attraction.name: Manneken Pis
attraction.description: Famous small bronze statue of a boy urinating.

attraction.name: Royal Palace
attraction.description: The official residence of the Belgian monarchs.

attraction.name: Cinquantenaire
attraction.description: Park featuring a triumphal arch and museums.

attraction.name: Mini-Europe
attraction.description: Park with miniature models of European landmarks.

attraction.name: Comic Strip Center
attraction.description: Museum dedicated to the history of Belgian comics.

attraction.name: Magritte Museum
attraction.description: Museum dedicated to the surrealist artist René Magritte.

attraction.name: Mont des Arts
attraction.description: Historic site with gardens and a panoramic city view.

attraction.name: St. Michael Cathedral
attraction.description: Gothic church located on the Treurenberg hill.

district.name: Pentagon
district.description: The Pentagon (French: Le Pentagone, Dutch: Vijfhoek) is the historical center of Brussels. It derives its name from the pentagonal shape of the inner beltway that surrounds the area. The Pentagon of Brussels encompasses many charming and beautiful attractions, with deeply ornate buildings on the Grand-Place/Grote Markt, and a fish-and-crustacean overdose of St. Catherine's Square (Place...

district.name: European Quarter
district.description: The European Quarter (French: Quartier européen, Dutch: Europese wijk) in Brussels, Belgium is a large office area east of the center which is mostly taken up by the European Union. The district also houses the large Parc de Cinquantenaire and a beautiful, but expensive, residential area on its northern edge. Metro line 5 goes straight through the district, and 6 also traverses the western edge....

district.name: West
district.description: The West contains the extensive western suburbs of Brussels. Except for the Sacred Heart basilica, tourists usually do not visit this part of the city. ]] * * * * * * Football: RSC Anderlecht play soccer in First Division A, the country's top tier. Their home ground is Lotto Park, capacity 22,500. * *

district.name: South
district.description: The South district in the Belgian capital of Brussels comprises the municipalities of Saint-Gilles / Sint-Gillis, Forest / Vorst and Uccle / Ukkel. Forest (pronounced with a silent "st") is the French name of one of the municipalities surrounding Brussels (the Flemish name is Vorst), known for its historically important abbey, a collection of art deco buildings and a major concert hall. Green and...

district.name: Centre
district.description: The Centre is the historic centre of Brussels, including the world's most beautiful town square, the Grand-Place which is listed as . is on the eastern end of the city centre. It is a terminus station for most regional (suburban) services and many other intercity services (including from the Netherlands) stop by. The city centre has two metro stations, both on lines 1/5 (both metro lines share...

city_report.description: Cuore politico dell'Unione Europea, Bruxelles gestisce una complessità demografica unica con politiche di inclusione avanzate. Il suo profilo è definito da un'offerta alberghiera di alto profilo e una rete di trasporti capillare.
city_report.wiki_intro: The Pentagon (French: Le Pentagone, Dutch: Vijfhoek) is the historical center of Brussels. It derives its name from the pentagonal shape of the inner beltway that surrounds the area. The Pentagon of Brussels encompasses many charming and beautiful attractions, with deeply ornate buildings on the Grand-Place/Grote Markt, and a fish-and-crustacean overdose of St. Catherine's Square (Place...
